actions.label: Risk assessment
actions.text:
MH risk assessment:
- Current intent / plan / means:
- Recent triggers:
- Protective factors:
- Past attempts / hospital admissions:
- Substance use:
- MH services involvement:
- Capacity:

Action:
- Same-day F2F vs phone vs crisis team
- Safety plan documented
- Safeguarding referral if children in household
- Follow-up timeframe agreed

actions.label: Safeguarding check
actions.text:
Always consider:
• Children / dependents in household → safeguarding referral threshold
• Mental Capacity Assessment
• Document risk assessment in full
• Crisis team contact readily available
• Clear follow-up plan with named clinician

actions.label: Documentation
actions.text:
Safeguarding considerations:
• Notify practice safeguarding lead
• MARAC / DASH risk assessment if DV suspected
• Code in record (Safeguarding adult / child concern)
• Document concerns verbatim
• Information sharing per local policy
• Children in household — separate referral if at risk

actions.label: Acute pathway
actions.text:
If acute / ongoing pain → 999.

If resolved:
• Onset, duration, character, radiation, exertion-related?
• Cardiac risk factors: age, smoker, FH, DM, lipids, BP, prior CVD
• Associated: SOB, sweating, N&V, syncope
• Differential: ACS, PE, dissection, pericarditis, GORD, MSK, anxiety

Thresholds:
• HEART score for risk stratification
• Same-day clinical assessment if any concerning features

actions.label: HEART score template
actions.text:
HEART score:
- History (suspicious 0/1/2):
- ECG (normal / non-spec / abnormal 0/1/2):
- Age (<45 / 45-64 / >65 = 0/1/2):
- Risk factors (count: 0 / 1-2 / 3+ or known CVD = 0/1/2):
- Troponin (norm / 1-3x / >3x ULN = 0/1/2):

Total: __
0-3 = low risk · 4-6 = moderate · 7-10 = high

actions.label: Emergency MRI
actions.text:
Suspected cauda equina = same-day emergency MRI.

Red flags (any):
• Bilateral sciatica
• Saddle anaesthesia / paraesthesia
• Bladder dysfunction (retention / overflow)
• Bowel incontinence / loss of anal tone
• Sexual dysfunction

Action:
• Discuss with on-call neurosurgery / spinal team
• Direct ED referral if no urgent MRI access
• Document neuro exam including PR if appropriate

actions.label: SAH pathway
actions.text:
Thunderclap headache → consider SAH.

• Maximum intensity within seconds-minutes
• Same-day CT head (CT >95% sensitive within 6h)
• If CT negative & onset <6h, may exclude
• If onset >6h or symptoms ongoing, LP at 12h

Other differentials: cervical artery dissection, RCVS, pituitary apoplexy, venous sinus thrombosis.

Action: same-day ED referral.

actions.label: Acute pathway
actions.text:
Suspected upper / lower GI bleed.

• Haemodynamics: HR, BP, lying/standing
• Anticoagulants / antiplatelets / NSAIDs
• Liver disease / known varices
• Glasgow-Blatchford for upper GI bleed

Action:
• Acute / unstable → 999
• Stable + suspicious → same-day medical admission
• Painless rectal bleeding > 50y → 2WW colorectal

actions.label: Glasgow-Blatchford template
actions.text:
Glasgow-Blatchford:
- Urea (mmol/L):
- Hb (g/L):
- Systolic BP:
- Pulse ≥100:
- Melaena:
- Syncope:
- Hepatic disease:
- Cardiac failure:

Score: __
0 = consider outpatient · ≥1 = admit

actions.label: DMARD monitoring
actions.text:
Methotrexate monitoring (NICE / BSR):

• FBC + U&E + LFT every 12 weeks (or 4-weekly if dose change / new abnormalities)
• Annual chest X-ray if respiratory symptoms
• Pneumococcal + annual flu vaccine
• Folic acid 5mg weekly (≥24h after MTX)

Warnings:
• Teratogenic — 6mo washout F, 3mo M before conception
• Hepatotoxic — alcohol limits
• Pulmonary toxicity — investigate any new SOB / dry cough
• Drug interactions — esp. trimethoprim, NSAIDs (caution)

actions.label: Bloods reminder
actions.text:
Methotrexate monitoring bloods:
- FBC + film:
- U&E:
- LFTs:
- Date of last MTX dose:
- Current dose:
- Folic acid:
- Symptoms? (SOB, mouth ulcers, infection):

Next review due:

actions.label: Lithium monitoring
actions.text:
Lithium monitoring (NICE):

• Level 12h post-dose, 3-monthly (more often if dose change, illness, drug interaction)
• U&E + TFT + Ca every 6 months
• Target range typically 0.6-0.8 mmol/L (0.4-1.0 acceptable)
• Toxicity above 1.5 mmol/L

Common traps:
• Dehydration / D&V → toxicity
• NSAIDs, ACEi, thiazides ↑ levels
• Pregnancy considerations — specialist advice
• Hypothyroidism long-term

actions.label: Anticoag review
actions.text:
Annual anticoagulant review:

• Indication still valid?
• Renal function — dose adjustment for DOACs
• Bleeding risk reassessment (HAS-BLED, ORBIT)
• CHA₂DS₂-VASc for AF (consider stopping if low risk)
• Patient knows what to do if a dose is missed
• Has Yellow Book / DOAC alert card
• Interacting meds review (esp. NSAIDs, antibiotics, antifungals)
• Bleeding episodes — frequency, severity, source
• Falls risk if elderly

actions.label: UTI history & exam
actions.text:
UTI history:
- Dysuria / frequency / urgency:
- Suprapubic pain / loin pain:
- Duration of symptoms:
- Fever / rigors / vomiting:
- Vaginal discharge:
- Sexually active / new partner:
- Pregnancy possible:
- Catheter / structural abnormality:
- Recent abx / hospitalisation:
- Recurrence (≥3 in 12mo or ≥2 in 6mo):
- Diabetes / immunosuppression:

O/E:
- Vitals (sepsis screen):
- Abdominal exam:
- Loin tenderness:
- (Dipstick: not for ≥65y or catheterised):

Plan:
- 1st line: nitrofurantoin or trimethoprim per local sensitivity
- 2nd line: pivmecillinam / fosfomycin
- Pyelonephritis features → admit / IV abx

actions.label: FeverPAIN scoring
actions.text:
FeverPAIN score (1 point each):
- Fever in last 24h:
- Purulence (tonsils):
- Attended within 3 days of onset:
- Inflamed tonsils (severe):
- No cough or coryza:

Total: __
0-1: 13-18% strep — no abx, self-care
2-3: 34-40% strep — delayed abx (3d) or no abx
4-5: 62-65% strep — immediate abx

Abx of choice: phenoxymethylpenicillin 500mg QDS 5-10d (clarithromycin if pen-allergic)

actions.label: OM history & exam
actions.text:
Otitis media history:
- Onset / duration:
- Fever / unwell:
- Otalgia laterality:
- Discharge / hearing loss:
- Recent URTI:
- Recurrent OM (≥3 in 6mo or ≥4/yr):
- Eardrum perforation history:
- Grommets:

O/E:
- TM appearance: bulging / erythematous / dull / perforated / effusion
- Mastoid tenderness:
- General — temp, fluid intake:

Plan:
- Most resolve in 3 days, paracetamol / ibuprofen
- Abx if: <2y bilateral, perforation + discharge, systemically unwell, deteriorating
- Amoxicillin 5-7d (clarithromycin if pen-allergic)
- Safety net: persistent fever 3-4d, mastoid swelling, hearing loss >2-4w

actions.label: Back pain assessment
actions.text:
Back pain — red flags (refer urgently):
- Cauda equina (saddle anaesthesia, bladder/bowel)
- Significant trauma
- Age <20 or >55 new onset
- Progressive neurological deficit
- Fever / weight loss / night pain (cancer/infection)
- IVDU / immunosuppression
- History of malignancy
- Severe progressive limb weakness

Yellow flags (psychosocial):
- Belief pain is harmful
- Avoidance behaviour
- Low mood / withdrawal
- Job dissatisfaction
- Compensation/legal issues

Management (no red flags):
- Stay active, return to normal activities
- Analgesia: NSAIDs first-line (with PPI cover if indicated)
- Avoid opioids and gabapentinoids for non-specific LBP
- Refer to physio / MSK if not improving in 4-6 weeks

actions.label: Resp red flags
actions.text:
Cough / breathlessness — concerning features:
• Haemoptysis
• Weight loss / night sweats
• Smoker / ex-smoker, esp. age >40
• Persistent cough >3 weeks
• Hoarseness >3 weeks
• Acute onset breathlessness (PE / pneumothorax)
• Tachypnoea, hypoxia, accessory muscle use
• Single-lobe / focal signs
• Failure to respond to abx

Thresholds:
• Same-day F2F if any red flag, hypoxia, or systemic unwellness
• 2WW chest if haemoptysis + age >40, or persistent cough + smoker

actions.label: Resp assessment
actions.text:
Respiratory presentation:
- Onset / duration:
- Cough: dry / productive (colour, consistency):
- Haemoptysis:
- Breathlessness — exertional / rest, MRC grade:
- Wheeze:
- Fever, night sweats, weight loss:
- Chest pain — pleuritic / cardiac:
- PE risk: travel, immobility, surgery, OCP, malignancy, leg swelling:
- Smoking pack-years:
- Asthma / COPD:

O/E:
- Vitals: HR, BP, RR, sats, temp:
- WOB / accessory muscles:
- Chest exam:
- PEFR (if asthma):

Plan:

actions.label: 7-point checklist
actions.text:
Weighted 7-point checklist for pigmented lesion (refer 2WW if score ≥3 OR any major):

Major (2 points each):
- Change in size:
- Irregular shape:
- Irregular colour:

Minor (1 point each):
- Largest diameter ≥7mm:
- Inflammation:
- Oozing / bleeding:
- Change in sensation (itch):

Total: __

Lesion description:
- Site:
- Diameter:
- Colour:
- Border:
- Evolution (timeline):
- Symptoms:
- ABCDE: Asymmetry / Border / Colour / Diameter / Evolving:

Photo on record? Y/N

actions.label: MH consultation
actions.text:
MH presentation:
- Onset / duration / triggers:
- Symptoms (mood, anhedonia, sleep, appetite, energy, concentration):
- Anxiety symptoms / panic:
- Functional impact (work, relationships, self-care):
- Risk: thoughts of self-harm / suicide / harm to others:
- Substance use:
- Past MH history / family history:
- Social context: housing, finances, support network:
- Recent losses / life events:
- PHQ-9: __ / GAD-7: __

Plan:
- Self-help / IAPT / counselling
- SSRI if moderate-severe (caution under 25 — review 2 weekly):
- Safety net + follow-up:

actions.label: Post-discharge checklist
actions.text:
After hospital discharge — review:

• Medication reconciliation (new / changed / stopped)
• Follow-up appointments booked?
• Outstanding investigations / results pending?
• Self-monitoring / safety-net advice given?
• District nurse / community support arranged?
• Social: home situation, carer, equipment
• Code recent admission in record
• Update problem list and registers (e.g. HF, COPD, AF post-MI)
• Consider proactive care plan if frail / multimorbid

actions.label: Post-discharge review
actions.text:
Post-discharge review:

Admission:
- Dates:
- Diagnoses:
- Procedures:
- Discharge medications (new/changed):
- Follow-up planned:

Review today:
- Recovery progress:
- Symptoms — concerning / improving:
- Pain control:
- Mobility / ADLs:
- Med compliance / side effects:
- Outstanding actions chased:
- Safety net advice:

actions.label: SMR template
actions.text:
Structured Medication Review (SMR):

Patient understanding:
- Knows why each med is prescribed:
- Adherence / compliance:
- Side effects experienced:
- Self-monitoring (BP, BG, peak flow):

For each medication:
- Indication still valid?
- Dose appropriate (renal/hepatic adjustment)?
- Duplications / interactions / cascades?
- Anticholinergic burden / falls risk?
- Recent monitoring done?

Deprescribe candidates:
- PPI > 8 weeks without indication
- Long-term benzo / Z-drug
- Statin in extreme frailty / limited life expectancy
- Anti-hypertensives if postural hypotension
- Aspirin without clear secondary prevention indication

Agreed plan:

actions.label: Fit note template
actions.text:
Med3 considerations:

- Diagnosis (the condition affecting work):
- Functional limitations (what can / can't do):
- Duration: <3mo (any duration) / >3mo (max 3mo):
- 'Not fit' or 'May be fit' (consider phased return / amended duties / altered hours / workplace adaptations):
- Assessment date and end date:
- Comments / advice to employer:

For mental health:
- Avoid jargon — concrete functional language
- Consider OH referral / IAPT signposting

For MSK:
- Active management plan
- Avoid bed rest > 1-2 days

actions.label: HRT consultation
actions.text:
Menopause / HRT consultation:

Symptoms:
- Vasomotor (flushes, sweats, night sweats):
- Mood, sleep, cognition:
- GU: vaginal dryness, dyspareunia, urinary:
- Joint / muscle pain:
- Libido:
- Functional impact:

History:
- LMP / cycle changes:
- Contraception still needed?
- Migraine with aura?
- VTE / clotting history (personal / family):
- Breast / endometrial / ovarian cancer history:
- BMI / smoking / alcohol:
- Cardiovascular risk:
- Liver disease:
- Current meds:

Discussion:
- Risk / benefit balance
- Options: sequential vs continuous combined; oestrogen-only if hysterectomy; transdermal preferred if VTE / migraine / hepatic / >60
- Vaginal oestrogen for GU symptoms (no extra systemic risk)
- Testosterone for low libido (off-label)
- Lifestyle, CBT for vasomotor

Plan / review timeframe:
